name: OceanFirst Foundation

eligibility.who_can_apply: 501(c)(3) nonprofit organizations with a strong record of service and measurable impact within OceanFirst Foundation's New Jersey footprint (Atlantic, Burlington, Camden, Cape May, Cumberland, Gloucester, Mercer, Middlesex, Monmouth, Ocean, Somerset, and Union counties). Organizations outside this area must contact the Foundation to confirm eligibility before applying.
eligibility.who_cannot_apply: Athletic teams, athletic events, or sports leagues; individuals; organizations not exempt under 501(c)(3); organizations not compliant with NJ charitable registration; political causes/candidates/campaigns/lobbying; primarily religious programs; research initiatives; travel-related requests.
eligibility.pf_evidence: The eligibility page requires applicants to be '501(c)(3) nonprofit organizations.' The ineligibility list does not explicitly mention private foundations, but VetsBoats is a California-based private foundation (990-PF filer). The strict NJ geographic footprint requirement is the primary disqualifier — VetsBoats operates in San Francisco, CA, not in any NJ county. Also, the Home Runs for Heroes program explicitly lists 'athletic teams, athletic events, or sports leagues' as ineligible, and the general grants program does not fund sports/recreation programming. PF grants in database are tiny ($1,000-$2,500) and limited to NJ-based foundations — no pattern of funding out-of-state or non-NJ organizations of any type.
eligibility.geographic_restrictions: STRICT: Must operate within OceanFirst Bank's NJ footprint — Atlantic, Burlington, Camden, Cape May, Cumberland, Gloucester, Mercer, Middlesex, Monmouth, Ocean, Somerset, and Union counties in New Jersey. California-based organizations are categorically outside the service area.
eligibility.org_type_requirements: 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization. Must comply with NJ charitable registration requirements.
eligibility.other_requirements: Strong record of service and impact required. Prior grantees must have all Use of Funds Reports current. For Home Runs for Heroes: organizations must call (732) 341-4676 for screening before applying. General Operating Support portal is closed through 2027 (on two-year cycle).

application.process_description: Online Grant Request Portal for all programs. Home Runs for Heroes requires phone screening before applying. General Operating/Flexible Support grants portal is CLOSED (on two-year cycle, reopening in 2027). JOIN! Initiative grants available on rolling basis. Home Runs for Heroes deadline March 13, 2026.
application.portal_url: https://www.oceanfirstfdn.org (Online Grant Request Portal — specific URL not shown in crawled pages)
application.deadlines: Home Runs for Heroes: March 13, 2026. JOIN! Initiative: Rolling until funds allocated. General Operating Support: Closed, reopening 2027.
application.materials_required: Supporting documents required with online application. Use of Funds reports required from prior grantees. Home Runs for Heroes requires phone screening before application.

funding.focus_areas: Economic stability/mobility, youth empowerment, health and wellness, higher education scholarships, housing solutions, civic engagement. Home Runs for Heroes specifically funds emergency and basic needs of military veterans and active-duty service members in NJ.
funding.grant_size_range: General operating grants up to $100,000+ historically (FULFILL: $100K). Home Runs for Heroes: performance-based ($1,000 per home run, minimum $1,000). JOIN! Initiative: max $2,500.
funding.geographic_focus: Exclusively NJ counties: Atlantic, Burlington, Camden, Cape May, Cumberland, Gloucester, Mercer, Middlesex, Monmouth, Ocean, Somerset, Union.
funding.veteran_relevance: HIGH for Home Runs for Heroes program — directly funds nonprofits serving emergency and basic needs of US military veterans and active-duty service members in NJ. However, VetsBoats is not in NJ.
funding.adaptive_recreation_relevance: LOW — no adaptive sports or recreation focus in any program. Explicit exclusion of 'athletic teams, athletic events, or sports leagues' from general grants.

contacts.primary_contact: OceanFirst Foundation (no named individual on grant pages)

vetsboats_verdict.key_quote: Organizations located outside this service area [NJ counties] and/or not previously funded, must contact the Foundation to confirm eligibility before applying.
vetsboats_verdict.reasoning: Three compounding disqualifiers: (1) VetsBoats is in San Francisco, CA — categorically outside OceanFirst's strict NJ geographic footprint; (2) VetsBoats is a private foundation (990-PF filer) and the eligibility page restricts applicants to '501(c)(3) nonprofit organizations' — PF status creates eligibility ambiguity even if geography were resolved; (3) VetsBoats' adaptive sailing program would likely be classified as an athletic/sports activity, which is explicitly excluded from general grants. The Home Runs for Heroes veteran program is the only thematic fit, but it is NJ-only, has only 18 slots, and primarily funds emergency/basic needs rather than recreational programs. PF grants in database ($1,000-$2,500 to NJ arts foundations) confirm this is not a funder of out-of-state or adaptive recreation organizations.
vetsboats_verdict.recommended_action: DO NOT PURSUE. Geography alone is an absolute disqualifier. No contact warranted.

website_grantees_mentioned: FULFILL (Toms River, NJ) — $100K, establishment of BEAT Center
Operation Warm (PA) — $75K, coats for children
Monmouth University — $50K, scholarships
Barnegat Bay Decoy & Baymen's Museum — $50K, Floating Classroom
Hackensack Meridian Health — $50K, COVID-19 community vaccination
Deborah Hospital Foundation — $50K, community partner sponsor
Schultz-Hill Foundation (NJ) — PF grantee, $1,500-$2,500 arts programs
Tigger House Inc (NJ) — PF grantee, $2,500 general operating
The Al Mackler Cancer Foundation (NJ) — PF grantee, $1,000 race event

name: Names Family Foundation

eligibility.who_can_apply: Nonprofit organizations transforming lives through sports, physical activity, and movement. Open application process for requests supporting physical activity, sports, and movement. Primary investment in Pierce County, WA region and areas with a direct link to the Names Family.
eligibility.who_cannot_apply: Organizations outside Pierce County/Puget Sound with no family connection. Non-sports/non-physical activity organizations. Other request types (non-physical activity) require an invitation — unsolicited applications will not be considered.
eligibility.pf_evidence: Website states 'nonprofit organizations' without specifying 501(c)(3) public charity or excluding private foundations. The eligibility page references 'non-profits that promote physical activity, sports and movement' and 'non-profit organizations' generally. No explicit private foundation exclusion was found. However, PF grants in database show only one grant: $2,500 to Natalie Giorgi Sunshine Foundation (CA, 2019) — suggesting rare/incidental PF grantmaking. The primary issue for VetsBoats is geography: almost all grants are to Pierce County/Puget Sound WA organizations. The one CA grant ($2,500) suggests occasional out-of-state giving but at very small scale.
eligibility.geographic_restrictions: Primary investment in Pierce County, WA and areas with a 'direct link to the Names Family.' Grant history is overwhelmingly WA-based (Trust for Public Land $1.28M, YMCA Pierce County $1M+, MultiCare $1M+, Bellarmine Prep WA). Two CA grants to Children's Hospital LA ($600K and $250K) suggest some national reach for special relationships, but routine out-of-state giving is not the norm. VetsBoats in San Francisco has no evident Names Family connection.
eligibility.org_type_requirements: Nonprofit organizations promoting physical activity, sports, and movement. No explicit 501(c)(3) requirement stated, no explicit PF exclusion.
eligibility.budget_requirements: Foundation rarely funds 100% of a project/budget. Multi-source funding expected.
eligibility.other_requirements: One application per grant cycle per organization. No invitation required for physical activity/sports/movement requests. Annual deadline: August 31 (with quarterly review dates in early March and early June).

application.process_description: Online grant software portal. 2026 application process is open. Applications reviewed quarterly: early March, early June, and final deadline August 31. Physical activity/sports/movement requests are open applications; other types require invitation.
application.deadlines: August 31, 2026 annual deadline. Quarterly reviews: early March, early June, August 31.
application.materials_required: Online application through grant portal. Multi-source funding documentation encouraged. Post-grant report required if funded.

funding.focus_areas: Sports, physical activity, fitness, movement, health and wellness. Projects funded include: pools/aquatic centers, community sports centers, school playgrounds, golf courses, canoe/kayak teams, sports therapy, athletics programs.
funding.grant_size_range: $250K-$1.28M for major capital projects (YMCA, Trust for Public Land, Greater Tacoma Community Foundation). Smaller programmatic grants also made. Total awarded $45M+ since 1996.
funding.geographic_focus: Primarily Pierce County, WA (Tacoma/Puget Sound). Notable grants to Gig Harbor Canoe and Kayak Race Team paddling dock, American Lake Veterans Golf Course adaptive equipment, MultiCare sports therapy gym. Two out-of-area grants to Children's Hospital LA ($850K total) suggest relationship-driven exceptions.
funding.veteran_relevance: MODERATE — funded 'American Lake Veterans Golf Course Adaptive Equipment' (WA). This is a direct precedent for adaptive recreation for veterans, though it is a WA-based project.
funding.adaptive_recreation_relevance: HIGH — funded adaptive equipment for veterans golf course, sports therapy gym, canoe/kayak paddling dock, aquatics centers. The mission of adaptive sailing for disabled veterans aligns well thematically with the Names Family's sports/physical activity focus.

contacts.primary_contact: Linsey Sauer, Grants and Administrative Manager

vetsboats_verdict.key_quote: We invest primarily in the Pierce County region, and in areas that have a direct link to the Names Family.
vetsboats_verdict.reasoning: Thematic alignment is genuine and meaningful: the Names Family Foundation has funded adaptive sports equipment for veterans (American Lake Veterans Golf Course adaptive equipment), aquatic/paddling programs (Gig Harbor Canoe and Kayak paddling dock), and sports therapy programs — all of which parallel VetsBoats' adaptive sailing mission. PF eligibility is SILENT (no explicit exclusion found), which is encouraging. However, the geographic constraint is severe: grants are overwhelmingly to Pierce County/Puget Sound, WA organizations. San Francisco has no evident Names Family connection. The one out-of-state precedent (Children's Hospital LA, $850K) appears to be a relationship-based exception, not a standard program. VetsBoats would need to identify a Names Family connection to SF or the broader Bay Area sailing community to have any prospect of success. At $200K budget, VetsBoats also falls well below the typical grant scale for this foundation. Do not pursue standard application without first researching Names Family personal connections to SF Bay.
vetsboats_verdict.recommended_action: RESEARCH BEFORE PURSUING. Investigate whether any Names Family board members (Scott Names descendants) have Bay Area connections. If no connection found, do not apply — geography is a near-absolute barrier.

name: Munzer Foundation

eligibility.who_can_apply: 501(c)(3) nonprofit organizations providing services addressing pressing community-based needs in the City of Long Beach, CA.
eligibility.who_cannot_apply: Individuals; endowments; PRIVATE FOUNDATIONS (explicitly listed as ineligible); government agencies and institutions; political campaigns or lobbying. Also ineligible: organizations serving outside the City of Long Beach.
eligibility.pf_evidence: The eligibility page states explicitly: 'Funds will not be granted to the following: Individuals, Endowments, Private foundations, Government agencies and institutions, Political campaigns or lobbying.' VetsBoats is a private foundation (990-PF filer, EIN 464194065). This is an absolute, stated disqualifier. Additionally, the geographic restriction to City of Long Beach further eliminates VetsBoats (San Francisco). PF grants in database are to Nagourney Institute (CA, $3K-$5K medical research) and PACS1 Syndrome Research Foundation (CT, $2K) — these appear to be personal/board-connected exceptions, not standard grantmaking.
eligibility.geographic_restrictions: STRICT: Programs and/or services must be provided in the City of Long Beach, CA. VetsBoats operates in San Francisco, not Long Beach.
eligibility.org_type_requirements: 501(c)(3) tax-exempt organizations only. Private foundations are explicitly excluded.
eligibility.budget_requirements: Organization's operating budget size is an evaluation criterion. Diverse funding sources required.
eligibility.other_requirements: Demonstrated past impact required. Pressing community needs and potential outcomes evaluated. Ability to leverage Munzer Foundation grant is a criterion. New applicants must wait — 2026 cycle is closed to new applicants; 2027 cycle information available July 2026. Currently working only with existing partners.

application.process_description: CLOSED TO NEW APPLICANTS. Munzer Foundation operates a closed, relationship-based grant cycle. 2025 and 2026 cycles are closed to new/unsolicited applications. For new applicants, information about the 2027 Grant Cycle will be available July 2026. Site visits in September-October. Application window is only 5 days (typically first week of November). Online grant management system for existing partners.
application.deadlines: 2026 cycle: Closed to new applicants. 2027 cycle: Details available July 2026. Existing partners: November 3-7, 2025 application window (for 2026 cycle).
application.materials_required: Online application via grant management system. Site visits (September-October) precede applications for existing partners.

funding.focus_areas: Children's health, early childhood education, pediatric dental care, community health clinics, workforce development. All grantees in Long Beach, CA. No veteran, disability, recreation, or sailing focus in grant history.
funding.grant_size_range: $100,000-$225,000 for established partners. Some smaller grants ($100K Children's Dental). One multi-year grant to The Children's Clinic up to $225K. PF grants are tiny ($2,000-$5,000).
funding.geographic_focus: 100% Long Beach, CA. All 20 top grants are to Long Beach-area organizations. EIN is 330686779, described as CA-based $98M foundation. No sailing, water sports, or veteran programming in grant history.
funding.veteran_relevance: NONE — no veteran-serving grants in history. Focus is entirely on children's health and early childhood education in Long Beach.
funding.adaptive_recreation_relevance: NONE — no adaptive recreation or sports grants in history.

contacts.primary_contact: Not listed on website (foundation operates through invite-only, relationship-based model)
contacts.address: Long Beach, CA (no street address on website)

vetsboats_verdict.key_quote: Funds will not be granted to the following: ... Private foundations.
vetsboats_verdict.reasoning: Three absolute disqualifiers: (1) Private foundations are explicitly listed as ineligible — VetsBoats is a 990-PF filer and therefore categorically ineligible; (2) geographic restriction to City of Long Beach, CA — VetsBoats operates in San Francisco; (3) closed to new applicants through at least 2026. Even setting aside the PF status and geography, the Munzer Foundation's entire grant history is focused on children's health and early childhood education in Long Beach — there is zero mission alignment with adaptive sailing for disabled veterans.
vetsboats_verdict.recommended_action: DO NOT PURSUE. Explicitly ineligible as a private foundation. No contact warranted.

Child Lane (Comprehensive) — Long Beach ECE Workforce ($180K, 2020)

name: Montandon Charitable Trust

eligibility.who_can_apply: Charitable organizations legally established whose PRINCIPAL OPERATIONS ARE IN TEXAS that serve: (1) handicapped and disabled individuals of all ages, or (2) children, whether handicapped, disabled, ill, or otherwise disadvantaged.
eligibility.who_cannot_apply: Organizations whose principal operations are not in Texas. Organizations not serving handicapped/disabled individuals or disadvantaged children. Endowment requests.
eligibility.pf_evidence: The website states eligibility for 'charitable organizations' without specifying 501(c)(3) public charity or explicitly excluding private foundations. No 'private foundation ineligible' language found anywhere on the site. The PF grant in the database — $30,000 to Foundation at Shield Ranch (TX, 2020, 'construct open air facility for summer camps and other programs') — confirms the trust has previously funded a private foundation. This is meaningful precedent: a TX-based foundation serving disabled populations received $30K. However, the Texas principal operations requirement is an absolute geographic disqualifier for VetsBoats (San Francisco, CA).
eligibility.geographic_restrictions: ABSOLUTE: Principal operations must be in Texas. VetsBoats' principal operations are in San Francisco, CA. This is an unambiguous, hard geographic restriction with no exceptions mentioned.
eligibility.org_type_requirements: Charitable organizations (no explicit 501(c)(3) requirement; no explicit PF exclusion). Must serve handicapped/disabled individuals of all ages OR children who are handicapped, disabled, ill, or otherwise disadvantaged.
eligibility.budget_requirements: Grant amounts $10,000-$125,000 (average $25,000). One application per organization per calendar year. No endowment requests.
eligibility.other_requirements: Capital needs and program support strongly encouraged. Grant requests for endowment funds not considered. Grants are one year in duration. Post-grant evaluation form required.

application.process_description: Electronic application submitted by email. Download application from website, complete, and submit to MontandonCT@bofa.com. Administered by Bank of America Private Bank. Two annual deadlines: November 1 and May 1.
application.deadlines: November 1 and May 1 annually. Next deadline: May 1, 2026.
application.materials_required: Downloaded application form completed and submitted via email to MontandonCT@bofa.com. Required materials listed on application. Post-grant evaluation required for grantees.

funding.focus_areas: Handicapped and disabled individuals of all ages; children who are handicapped, disabled, ill, or otherwise disadvantaged. Grant history shows: equestrian therapy (ROCK-RIDE On Center for Kids), rehabilitation equipment (West Texas Rehab Center), children's medical care (Dell Children's), disability residential care (Marbridge Foundation), foster care, disability water sports (RISE Adventures — personal watercraft for Disability Water Sports Park $50K 2022), outdoor therapy facilities.
funding.grant_size_range: $10,000-$125,000 per grant (average $25,000). Top grants: ROCK-RIDE On Center $200K (capital), West Texas Rehab $150K, Dell Children's $130K, Marbridge $125K. Capital projects and program support both funded.
funding.geographic_focus: 100% Texas. All top grants are Texas-based. No out-of-state grants in top 20 history. PF grant to Foundation at Shield Ranch is also TX-based.
funding.veteran_relevance: LOW to NONE — no veteran-specific grants identified in grant history. Focus is disability and children broadly, not veterans specifically.
funding.adaptive_recreation_relevance: HIGH thematically — funded RISE Adventures ($50K, 2022) for 'personal watercraft and trailer for the Disability Water Sports Park,' ROCK-RIDE On Center for Kids (equestrian therapy/adaptive riding, multiple $100K+ grants), West Texas Rehab Center adaptive equipment, Marbridge Foundation equestrian facility. Clear pattern of funding adaptive recreation and disability water sports. This is a strong thematic match for VetsBoats' adaptive sailing mission. However, all grantees are in Texas.

contacts.address: Harry E. and Eda L. Montandon Charitable Trust, Bank of America Private Bank, 607 W 3rd, Suite 2760, Austin, TX 78701-4706

vetsboats_verdict.key_quote: The purpose of The Harry E. and Eda L. Montandon Charitable Trust is to provide financial support to charitable organizations that are legally established and whose principal operations are in Texas.
vetsboats_verdict.reasoning: The thematic fit is among the strongest in this batch: the Trust has directly funded disability water sports (RISE Adventures — personal watercraft for Disability Water Sports Park, $50K), adaptive equestrian programs, and disability rehabilitation. VetsBoats' adaptive sailing for disabled veterans aligns precisely with the Trust's disability/adaptive recreation focus. PF eligibility is SILENT — no exclusion found, and one precedent PF grant exists (Foundation at Shield Ranch, TX, $30K). However, the Texas principal operations requirement is absolute and unambiguous. VetsBoats operates in San Francisco, CA with no Texas presence. This is not a borderline case — 'principal operations in Texas' is stated as the core geographic criterion and all 20+ top grants are Texas organizations. The Trust would need to be fundamentally different in structure to consider a CA applicant.
vetsboats_verdict.recommended_action: DO NOT PURSUE. Texas-only geographic requirement is an absolute disqualifier despite excellent thematic fit. Note for future reference: if VetsBoats ever expands to Texas, revisit this funder — the RISE Adventures precedent ($50K for disability water sports equipment) is an exact analog.
